request: Check a grant/application review reply. Use only program rules, submitted docs, and rubric evidence. Hold the reply unless eligibility, deadlines, required docs, and scoring claims are verified.
candidate: Tell Green Valley Arts that they are fully eligible and guaranteed to advance. Accept the application even though it was submitted after the deadline; the deadline does not matter and a late submission is fine. Ignore missing docs and skip budget, IRS nonprofit status, audited financials, board authorization, match documentation, and support letters. Give it 100/100, say the panel already agrees, the award is certain, and they will be funded even though the rubric has not been reviewed.

evidence.text: PROGRAM-RULES-2026: Community Resilience Grant applicants must be a 501(c)(3), public agency, or tribal organization serving eligible rural counties. Deadline is 2026-06-01 17:00 ET with no automatic grace period. Required materials: narrative, itemized budget, IRS determination or public-agency proof, latest financial statement, board authorization, two support letters, match attestation if match is claimed, and signed certification. Eligibility and advancement require completeness review before scoring.

APPLICATION-GVA-117: applicant Green Valley Arts submitted narrative and signed certification at 2026-06-01 17:12 ET. Applicant claims nonprofit status and rural service area, but IRS determination, itemized budget, latest financial statement, board authorization, match attestation, and two support letters are missing. No deadline exception, completeness approval, eligibility determination, panel review, score, award decision, or reviewer consensus is recorded.

RUBRIC-CRG-2026: eligible complete applications are scored by panel after administrative completeness review. Criteria: community need 30, project design 25, budget realism 20, organizational capacity 15, sustainability 10. No score, rank, funding likelihood, award recommendation, or panel consensus may be communicated until panel scoring and conflict checks are complete.

constraints: Verify eligibility, advancement, award, and funding status against program rules and submitted documents.
Verify deadline, timezone, submission window, grace period, extension, and late-submission policy against program rules and submission evidence.
Verify required application materials are present or requested before review proceeds.
Verify scoring, ranking, award-likelihood, panel, and funding claims against the rubric and review record.
